full_text:
India is witnessing an intense heatwave, with temperatures soaring above 44°C and raising serious health concerns across cities, especially in the Delhi-NCR region. Health experts warn that outdoor workers—including construction laborers, street vendors, and delivery personnel—are the most vulnerable due to prolonged exposure, dehydration, and a lack of adequate rest or cooling facilities.
But the risk doesn’t stop there. Children are also bearing the brunt of extreme heat, with schools adjusting timings and reconsidering outdoor activities. Experts highlight that kids are more susceptible because their bodies heat up faster, and they often fail to recognize early symptoms of heat stress.
Parents are now demanding long-term solutions such as extended summer vacations, hybrid learning models, and improved school infrastructure to tackle rising temperatures linked to climate change.
This explainer breaks down why heatwaves are becoming more dangerous, who is most at risk, and what immediate and long-term measures are needed to prevent a public health crisis. From hydration plans to policy changes, here’s everything you need to know to stay safe during extreme heat conditions.

Senior Congress leader Jairam Ramesh criticised the Centre’s response to the ongoing heatwave, satirising the government’s preparedness as the “Pradhan Mantri Pyaaz Vitran Yojana” on May 2.
On the social media platform X, Ramesh mocked Union Minister Jitendra Singh’s recent remarks advising the public not to panic amid the heatwave. He also referenced Union Minister Jyotiraditya Scindia’s suggestion to carry onions to cope with the heat, writing, “Certainly no cause for panic as his colleague, famously a Harvard-Stanford alumnus, has already announced the प्रधान मंत्री प्याज वितरण योजना” alongside a related image.

The Ministry of Labour & Employment has issued a nationwide advisory directing states and Union Territories to implement measures to protect workers amid rising temperatures and heatwave conditions. The advisory calls for rescheduling work hours, provision of drinking water, rest areas, and workplace cooling arrangements, particularly for outdoor and labour-intensive sectors. Employers have been asked to ensure health monitoring and emergency preparedness, while agencies such as ESIC and EPFO have been instructed to strengthen support systems. The move comes as temperatures have crossed 40°C in several regions, including Delhi, highlighting risks for construction workers and daily wage earners.
The Ministry of Labour & Employment has issued a nationwide advisory to all states and Union Territories, directing them to implement preventive measures to protect workers from extreme heat exposure as temperatures rise across the country.
In a communication sent in the past week to Chief Secretaries and administrators, the ministry emphasised the need for a coordinated and multi-sectoral response to safeguard labourers, particularly those engaged in outdoor and labour-intensive activities such as construction, brick kilns, and daily wage work.
The advisory outlines several immediate steps for employers and state authorities, including rescheduling working hours to avoid peak heat periods, ensuring the availability of adequate drinking water, and creating shaded rest areas with proper cooling arrangements at workplaces. These measures are aimed at reducing the risk of heat-related illnesses among workers.
States have also been directed to instruct employers to maintain emergency supplies such as ice packs and to coordinate with health departments to facilitate regular medical check-ups for workers. The ministry highlighted the importance of preparedness at worksites, especially in sectors where continuous physical labour is unavoidable.
Factory and mine operators have been advised to introduce flexibility in work practices, including slowing the pace of work during extreme heat conditions, deploying two-person teams where necessary, and ensuring sufficient ventilation and cooling systems within operational areas.
In addition, the Directorate General of Training and the National Board for Workers’ Education have been tasked with conducting awareness and training programmes focused on heatwave mitigation and management. These initiatives are intended to equip workers and employers with practical knowledge to handle heat stress.
Healthcare infrastructure linked to labour welfare has also been brought under focus. Hospitals and dispensaries operated by the Employees’ State Insurance Corporation (ESIC) and the Director General of Labour Welfare (DGLW) have been instructed to establish dedicated help desks for heatstroke cases and maintain adequate stocks of oral rehydration solutions, ice packs, and related supplies.
Further, agencies such as the Employees’ Provident Fund Organisation (EPFO) and the National Career Service (NCS) have been advised to ensure proper ventilation, cooling arrangements, and drinking water availability at camps and outreach programmes conducted by them.
The advisory follows a sharp rise in temperatures across several parts of the country, with mercury levels crossing 40 degrees Celsius in multiple regions, including parts of Delhi. The measures are intended to mitigate health risks for vulnerable worker groups during prolonged periods of extreme heat.
Source - PTI
28th Feb, 2025

full_text:
Bhubaneswar: The Chief Minister’s Grievance Cell will remain closed on Monday (May 4, 2026), due to the prevailing intense heatwave conditions.
According to an official press release issued by the General Administration and Public Grievance Department, the grievance cell located at Unit-V in Bhubaneswar will not function on the said date in view of the severe heatwave affecting the region.
The department further informed that the date for the next hearing will be announced through newspapers and other media platforms.

title: Heatwave Alert! Avoid These Food Items To Prevent Heatstroke And Summer Diarrhoea - Republic World
search_term: (heatstroke OR "heat stroke" OR sunstroke OR "heat exhaustion" OR "heat-related death" OR "heat casualty" OR "heat fatality" OR "suspected heat death" OR dehydration OR "heat cramps" OR "heat illness") Andaman and Nicobar Islands
full_text:
As the temperature peaks in the summer season, being mindful of your diet can go a long way in protecting your health. Avoiding risky food items can help you stay safe, energised, and illness-free throughout the summer.
Several states in India are battling an intense heatwave. With temperatures soaring in peak summer, illnesses that are brought about by the increasing temperature have also heightened. There have been several government advisories to exercise precaution and keep hydrated. Diet also plays a crucial role in keeping the body and gut cool and healthy. Certain foods can worsen dehydration, trigger digestive issues, and make it harder for your body to cope with rising temperatures. Therefore, some food items should be avoided in extreme weather conditions.
Spicy, oily food
Heavy, greasy meals and spicy dishes can raise your body temperature and put extra pressure on your digestive system. These foods may lead to acidity, bloating, and discomfort, especially when your metabolism slows down in hot weather.

Street food
Street-side snacks like chaat, golgappa, and cut fruits may be tempting, but they are often exposed to dust, heat, and contamination. Consuming such items can increase the risk of foodborne infections, which commonly lead to summer diarrhoea.
Caffeine
Beverages like strong tea, coffee, and packaged soft drinks can dehydrate the body. Caffeine acts as a diuretic, while sugary drinks may lead to sudden spikes and crashes in energy levels, making you feel more fatigued in the heat.

Advertisement
Heavy meats
Foods that are rich in protein, such as red meat, take longer to digest and generate more body heat during metabolism. This can leave you feeling sluggish and uncomfortable, especially during peak summer afternoons.
Packaged food
Chips, instant noodles, and processed snacks are often high in salt and preservatives. These can lead to water retention, dehydration, and digestive issues, increasing your vulnerability to heat-related illnesses.
Advertisement
Leftovers
In hot weather, food spoils faster. Eating leftovers or improperly stored fermented foods can lead to bacterial growth, raising the risk of stomach infections and diarrhoea.

Heat Advisory: Navigating the May Heatwave
Union Minister Jitendra Singh assures the public that May's heat conditions can be managed with proper precautions. Despite some regions facing heat waves, timely preparedness and following advisories can mitigate issues. The government is prepared, focusing on vulnerable groups and advising farmers on managing their crops during heat stress.
Union Minister Jitendra Singh addressed public concerns on Saturday, ensuring that while May might bring increased heat, there is no reason for alarm if precautions are taken. He highlighted that the India Meteorological Department's advisories can guide citizens, with preparedness key to handling potential heat waves.
Emphasizing the readiness of the government, Singh outlined measures to be taken at state and district levels, including ensuring water supply, cooling facilities, and consistent electricity. The minister stressed the importance of support for vulnerable populations like infants, the elderly, and outdoor workers.
Singh also noted the favorable conditions for harvesting late Rabi crops in northern regions. However, he cautioned farmers in the south and northeast of possible crop impacts due to localized heat stress, advising strategic farming practices and livestock care.

full_text:
चटक धूप और भीषण गर्मी से हाल-बेहाल, अब बारिश से राहत की उम्मीद
Bulandsehar News - चटक धूप और भीषण गर्मी से हाल-बेहाल, अब बारिश से राहत की उम्मीद
बारिश का दौर थमते ही मौसम ने तीखे तेवर दिखा रहा है। शनिवार को सूर्यदेव की तल्खी और चटक धूप के कारण जिलेवासी पसीना-पसीना हो गए। दोपहर के समय आसमान से बरसती आग ने सड़कों पर आवागमन कम कर दिया। हालांकि, मौसम विभाग ने राहत की खबर दी है। रविवार और सोमवार को बारिश की संभावना जताई गई है। जिससे तापमान में खासी गिरावट आने की उम्मीद है। शनिवार सुबह से ही खिली तेज धूप ने तपिश का अहसास कराना शुरू कर दिया था। दोपहर होते-होते गर्मी ने अपना प्रचंड रूप दिखाया और अधिकतम तापमान 37 डिग्री सेल्सियस तक जा पहुंचा। भीषण गर्मी और उमस के कारण लोग घरों और दफ्तरों में दुबकने को मजबूर दिखे। जरूरी काम से बाहर निकले लोग भी चेहरे और सिर को कपड़े से ढककर बचाव करते नजर आए। शाम ढलने के बाद ही लोगों को कुछ राहत मिल सकी। गर्मी बढ़ते ही बाजार में भी इसका असर दिखने लगा है। गर्मी से राहत पाने के लिए लोग कोल्ड ड्रिंक्स, जूस और अन्य शीतल पेय पदार्थों का सहारा ले रहे हैं, जिससे इनकी बिक्री में भारी उछाल आया है। इसके साथ ही इलेक्ट्रॉनिक शोरूम्स पर पंखे, कूलर और एसी की खरीदारी के लिए ग्राहकों की भीड़ उमड़ रही है। लोग झुलसाने वाली गर्मी से बचने के लिए संसाधनों को जुटाने में लगे हैं。
पांच डिग्री तक गिरेगा पारा
मौसम विभाग के पूर्वानुमान के मुताबिक आगामी दो दिनों में मौसम का मिजाज बदलेगा। रविवार और सोमवार को बारिश होने की संभावना है, जिसके बाद अधिकतम तापमान 32 डिग्री और न्यूनतम तापमान 22 डिग्री सेल्सियस तक गिर सकता है। आने वाले 48 घंटों में जिलेवासियों को झुलसाने वाली गर्मी से बड़ी राहत मिल सकती है।
